On site with Renown to perform motor winding analysis. Could not find any issues during Hi-pot testing. Will continue to investigate.

Upon responding to the pump failure at King city we were able to observe pump 1 running in auto. Pump 2, Pump 3 both faulted at the drive displaying a F37 fault which means one of two things. Phase loss or Phase imbalance. We were able to check the line voltage and it was above 600v and around 350 phase to ground on each phase. We reset Pump 2 and let it Run through a cycle. The output off the drive was 350v on each phase to ground which is right around where it should be while operating. The current across the phases was put by over 20a between phase B -A,C (A128 B108 C127) this leads me to believe the windings on the motor have seen spikes and are deteriorating . While operating the soft start is displaying A code of A37
Which, according to the manual means there is a current imbalance on the motor, should the imbalance go outside this threshold.  It will give us the same F 37 fault That is displayed during a phase loss which we were able to see on Scada here this morning. 

We performed tests on pump three and then pump one and all three pumps are giving us the same information. I've informed Bill and we will be looking at servicing these pump motors as soon as possible

FYI, Ernie and I came to site this morning for some follow up.

In an effort to determine if the temperature?sensing was working correctly we disabled the heat trace and flushed the sense line until the temperature stabilized, around 5C. The temperature was corroborated with an IR camera. Seems like the temperature sensors are working correctly. Heat trace re-enabled.

We verified that the temperature sensors are installed properly. As to their location, they are right adjacent to each other, ?not sure this is the best design? If they were separated by a greater distance the system may get a better average reading? However, the sensing line is long, and the temperature sensor only reports the temperature at that one signal spot. And I did not witness the heat trace installation. It is possible there is a cold spot somewhere along the run that the temperature sensors cannot see.

We set the set point to 10C.

We found the low temp alarm set point at 1C, too low, increased it to 5C.

Lastly, there is a small line tapped off the main sensing line in the valve room which goes to the sample sink. We cracked its ball valve so there is a small amount of flow.

Onsite to investigate tower level rising even though duty is off. Temperature outside is -3 degrees Celsius and has been colder all day. 
Found LIT sample line heat trace 3 off and reading 12 degrees but line is frozen. 
Set heat trace to heat to 14 degrees. 
Flushed LIT line. Lit line now matches mainline pressure and is reading accurately. 
No issues remain. Will have maintenance investigate the temperature reading on thermometer heat heat trace controller on Monday.
